text.title: user-interviews Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for user-interviews as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Poor recruitment — interviewing participants who do not match the target user profile.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Insights from the wrong users produce design decisions that serve a population that does not exist or will not use the product.

failure-mode.description: Not probing — accepting surface-level answers without follow-up.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: First answers are often rehearsed or socially acceptable; the real insight lives in the follow-up ("why is that?", "can you give me an example?").

failure-mode.description: No analysis — collecting data without synthesizing it into insights and sharing with the team.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unanalyzed interview data has zero design impact; synthesis is not optional.

failure-mode.description: Using agent-generated interview guides without neutrality review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated guides tend toward closed or mildly leading questions when product context is provided. Require explicit review for question neutrality before use.

failure-mode.description: Expecting agents to conduct interviews themselves.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent cannot replicate a human facilitator's rapport, body language reading, or real-time probing judgment. Interviews must be human-facilitated.

failure-mode.description: Relying on agent synthesis without validation against source notes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Human-in-loop checkpoint: insight statements must be validated against source notes by the researcher before being shared with stakeholders or used for design decisions.

failure-mode.description: Asking agent to "find examples" without providing verbatim transcript text.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent may hallucinate quotes if asked to "find examples" without being given the actual transcript text. Always provide verbatim source material.

failure-mode.description: Passing unredacted transcripts to external LLM APIs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Privacy: interview transcripts often contain PII. Ensure transcripts are sanitized (participant names replaced with IDs) before passing to external LLM APIs.
